refs.description: Go standard library's stringFinder implements Boyer-Moore with explicit bad-character and good-suffix skip tables, used by strings.Index for multi-byte patterns.
CPython's core string search (str.find, str.index, the in operator) uses a Boyer-Moore/Horspool hybrid with a Bloom-filter skip table, falling back to Two-Way for the worst case.
Educational, self-contained Python implementation of Boyer-Moore using the bad-character heuristic.
